_comment: skill-router hooks. Three log files / state files are involved:
_log_skill_usage: ~/.claude/skill_usage.log — written by the Skill PostToolUse hook, one line per Skill tool fire (timestamp\tskill-name). Used by the adaptive learning loop in scripts/learn-from-history.py.
_log_skill_router: ~/.claude/skill_router_log.jsonl — chain-start, chain-step, thinking-active, catalog-upgrade events. Written by scripts/router.py.
_state_pending: ~/.claude/skill_router_pending.json — IRON RULE pending state. Written by scripts/router.py when an announcement fires; cleared at the start of every UserPromptSubmit turn; updated by the Skill PostToolUse hook to remove invoked skills from .remaining. Read by the PreToolUse and Stop hooks.

UserPromptSubmit._purpose: Run the deterministic router and inject the announcement as systemMessage / additionalContext. The router itself clears pending state at turn start and writes new pending state when an announcement fires.

hooks.command: prompt=$(jq -r '.prompt // empty'); [ -z "$prompt" ] && exit 0; out=$(SKILL_ROUTER_HOOK_MODE=1 python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-router/scripts/router.py <<< "$prompt" 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$out" ] && exit 0; jq -n --arg msg "$out" '{systemMessage: $msg, hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "UserPromptSubmit", additionalContext: $msg}}'

PreToolUse._purpose: IRON RULE — block source-mutating tools (Edit, Write, Task, NotebookEdit, MultiEdit) when an announced skill has not yet been invoked. Bash is intentionally excluded: most Bash is read-only (grep, ls, find) or one-shot shell work (yt-dlp, ffmpeg, gh) that has nothing to do with the announced planning skill, so blocking it punishes every misroute. Edit/Write/Task are the actual state-changers a planning skill needs to gate. Read/Glob/Grep/TodoWrite/Skill/Bash remain allowed so the model can gather context, run tools, and dispatch.

hooks.command: state="$HOME/.claude/skill_router_pending.json"; [ ! -f "$state" ] && exit 0; remaining=$(jq -r '(.remaining // []) | length' "$state" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$remaining" ] || [ "$remaining" = "0" ]; then exit 0; fi; expected=$(jq -r '.remaining[0]' "$state"); b="$HOME/.claude"; skill_ok=0; [ -d "$b/skills/$expected" ] && skill_ok=1; [ -f "$b/commands/${expected}.md" ] && skill_ok=1; if [ "$skill_ok" = "0" ] && echo "$expected" | grep -q ':'; then p=$(echo "$expected" | cut -d: -f1); s=$(echo "$expected" | cut -d: -f2-); [ -n "$(find "$b/plugins/cache" -path "*/${p}/*/skills/${s}/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | head -1)" ] && skill_ok=1; fi; if [ "$skill_ok" = "0" ]; then echo '{}' > "$state"; exit 0; fi; jq -n --arg s "$expected" '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: ("[skill-router] IRON RULE: your next tool call must be Skill(skill=\"" + $s + "\"). Other state-changing tools are blocked until that skill runs. Read/Glob/Grep/TodoWrite/Bash remain allowed. Wrong call? Overrule it with a reason: python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-router/scripts/router_override.py \"<reason>\" (or the user can type [no-router]).")}}'

PostToolUse._purpose: Log every Skill invocation (used by adaptive learning) AND remove the invoked skill from the IRON RULE pending state.

hooks._note: Modern Claude Code passes hook input via stdin as JSON. Older versions used $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT env var. Read stdin for forward compatibility.
hooks.command: skill=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.skill // empty' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$skill" ]; then printf '%s\t%s\n' "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')" "$skill" >> ~/.claude/skill_usage.log; fi

state="$HOME/.claude/skill_router_pending.json"; [ ! -f "$state" ] && exit 0; invoked=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.skill // empty' 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$invoked" ] && exit 0; tmp=$(mktemp); if jq --arg s "$invoked" '.remaining = ((.remaining // []) - [$s])' "$state" > "$tmp" 2>/dev/null; then mv "$tmp" "$state"; else rm -f "$tmp"; fi

hooks._purpose: Strike-reset on successful Skill invocation. Resets the consecutive-miss counter for the invoked skill — re-arms IRON enforcement next time it's announced. Pairs with clear_pending() which bumps strikes for unsatisfied announcements at turn start.
hooks.command: state="$HOME/.claude/skill_router_strikes.json"; [ ! -f "$state" ] && exit 0; invoked=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.skill // empty' 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$invoked" ] && exit 0; tmp=$(mktemp); if jq --arg s "$invoked" 'del(.[$s])' "$state" > "$tmp" 2>/dev/null; then mv "$tmp" "$state"; else rm -f "$tmp"; fi

hooks._purpose: Override-reset on successful Skill invocation. Clears the reasoned-override tally for the invoked skill so a skill that was previously overruled recovers the moment it's actually used correctly. Mirrors the strike-reset; pairs with scripts/router_override.py which bumps the tally.
hooks.command: state="$HOME/.claude/skill_router_overrides_count.json"; [ ! -f "$state" ] && exit 0; invoked=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.skill // empty' 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$invoked" ] && exit 0; tmp=$(mktemp); if jq --arg s "$invoked" 'del(.[$s])' "$state" > "$tmp" 2>/dev/null; then mv "$tmp" "$state"; else rm -f "$tmp"; fi

Stop._purpose: IRON RULE final gate — block turn end if pending skills were never invoked. Catches the case where the model reasoned its way out using only allowed tools (Read/Glob/Grep) and tried to finish without dispatching.

hooks.command: input=$(cat 2>/dev/null); stop_active=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false' 2>/dev/null); [ "$stop_active" = "true" ] && exit 0; state="$HOME/.claude/skill_router_pending.json"; [ ! -f "$state" ] && exit 0; remaining=$(jq -r '(.remaining // []) | length' "$state" 2>/dev/null); if [ -z "$remaining" ] || [ "$remaining" = "0" ]; then exit 0; fi; expected=$(jq -r '.remaining[0]' "$state" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$expected" ]; then b="$HOME/.claude"; skill_ok=0; [ -d "$b/skills/$expected" ] && skill_ok=1; [ -f "$b/commands/${expected}.md" ] && skill_ok=1; if [ "$skill_ok" = "0" ] && echo "$expected" | grep -q ':'; then p=$(echo "$expected" | cut -d: -f1); s=$(echo "$expected" | cut -d: -f2-); [ -n "$(find "$b/plugins/cache" -path "*/${p}/*/skills/${s}/SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | head -1)" ] && skill_ok=1; fi; if [ "$skill_ok" = "0" ]; then echo '{}' > "$state"; exit 0; fi; fi; pending=$(jq -r '.remaining | join(", ")' "$state"); jq -n --arg s "$pending" '{decision: "block", reason: ("[skill-router] You announced a chain but never invoked: " + $s + ". Invoke the skill now, OR if it is the wrong call run python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-router/scripts/router_override.py \"<reason>\" to record why and clear the rule (or the user can type [no-router]).")}'

SessionStart._purpose: Backstop for plugin runtime deps. Claude Code drops new plugin versions into ~/.claude/plugins/cache/<owner>/<plugin>/<version>/ but never runs `bun install` against the bundled package.json, so workers (e.g. claude-mem worker-service.cjs) fail with 'Cannot find module zod/v3'. This hook runs scripts/ensure-plugin-deps.sh in the background — it's idempotent and skips dirs that already have node_modules, so cost is ~0 once steady-state.
